# Extended Case References — Lecture zKu_AK_57lM

## Cases mentioned in passing

### Forging press casting repair
- **canonical_cluster_id:** "Forging press casting repair"
- **Anchor:** `§1.p12`
- **Quote (layer 3):** "You do have to worry about different geometries — convergent heat flow and divergent heat flow. In convergent heat flow, I'm heating on the outside of a surface with a smaller surface on the inside — I might be heating the outside of a tube. I'll heat up faster because I have a smaller area to accumulate the heat in. In divergent heat flow, I'm heating on the inside of a tube or a sphere, and I have to heat up a greater mass out here. The area is decreasing as 1/r in convergent, increasing as 1/r in divergent; in a sphere it's 1/r² either way. Convergent will be faster heating, divergent will..."
